title: iGLUT and iGABA dissociation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9qmk6","text":"Dissociation of NPCs, immature and mature iGLUTs and iGABAs to viable single cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"en27k","text":"Set up at least twice (1 week separated from each other). You will appreciate the backup.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ce6ii","text":"Don’t forget to add DNAse; make sure papain is not expired.
 ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7bnbp","text":"Incubating longer than 15 min decreases cell viability and doesn't improve singleness of cells.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9s6p5","text":"If you are working with a lot of plates, you can stop the enzyme action after 10 mins papain incubation, and manually break down the cells afterward.
 ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2s492","text":"Incubate at 39C NOT 37C! It increases efficiency of dissociation and use a shaker in slow speed 125 rpm.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ep2v9","text":"DO NOT WASH CELLS IN NEURON MEDIA.  Use DMEM+FBS to stop enzymatic dissociation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7ilk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bjrfv","text":"Spin cells at low speed 600g NOT 1 krcf. They will clump otherwise.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6miq8","text":"Before straining, wait a bit for cell debris to settle, take the cells, and count that.
 ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b1a4n","text":"Don't overdilute your cells: 10x will fail if too diluted.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"42iiq","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":0,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8o13q","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7n53h","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"scfoi","text":"Papain Suspension 42.0 mg/mL (Worthington cat no. LS003127)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dvm0x","text":"Hank’s balanced salt solution (HBSS) with added HEPES","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pxuth","text":"DMEM (ThermoFisher Scientific, #10566-016)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"glqxj","text":"FBS (Gibco 10438-026) 0.5M","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2nckl","text":"EDTA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Aspire medium
Wash wells with PBS-EDTA 0.5mM (e.g., 0.5 mL per well of a 12-well-plate; recline the cells from tube rack to disturb the neurons as little as possible). Aspirate.
Add 1/4 of well volume of papain-HBSS-EDTA-HEPES solution (e.g., 250 µL per well of a 12-well-plate or 125 µL per well of a 24-well-plate)
Incubate at 37°C for 10-15 min in shaker at 125 rpm (bacterial shaker taped or inside metal holders). Tap cells on the side every 5 min.
Pipette cell clumps up and down to break cells.
Transfer dissociated cells to a 15 mL tube plate with 6 mL of DMEM-10%FBS (2 volume dilution of papain) and wash wells with 5 mL (e.g., 400 µL /well of a 12-well-plate)
3 mL cells suspension+ 6 mL Medium+ 5 mL Medium wash for each plate.
NOTE: There should almost no cell clumps.
Spin at 600g, 5 min, RT. Decant medium by inverting the tube.
Resuspend in 1-2 mL of DMEM-10%FBS/plate.
Pass cells through strainer on top of a 15 mL tube.
Count cells in hiPSCs setting
Wash w. PBS ((-Ca/-Mg) twice
0.5 mL Accutase, w/Rock inhibitor, 37°C, ~25 min, frequent agitation
Pipet gently w. p1000,
Wash medium w/FBS, and add 15 mL conical tube,
Spin, 300 g, 5min, 4°C, aspirate sup,
Re-suspended in neuron media w. 10% FBS and Ri.
Spin, 300 g, 5min, RT, remove sup
Add 100 µL staining buffer (2%BSA/0.02% tween20, RNAase inhibitor)
Add 10 µL FcX. resuspend, 10min, 4C (Fc block)
Add 2 µL (1µg) hashing Ab for each sample, 30min, @4C
Wash w.1 mL staining buffer, spin, 300 g, 5 min,
Repeat washing 3 times.
Ensure >60% viability
Counting: 2.5 µL cell suspension + 2.5 µL staining buffer (1:2 dilution) + 5 µL Trypanblue
creator: Kristen Brennand
